title: agropyron boreale subsp. alaskanum
intro: Agropyron boreale subsp. alaskanum, also known as Alaskan blue wheat grass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Agropyron boreale. This plant is believed to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. The health benefits of Alaskan blue wheat grass include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as a tea or infused in water to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
